metadata.rationale: 该样本的核心新闻事件系虚构：可用素材与原始证据一致指向的事实恰恰是2026年3月8日美国大多数地区仍按惯例在凌晨2点进入夏令时，而非被白宫紧急叫停。与此同时，文中多张图片并非真实记录这一“取消夏令时”事件的新闻现场，而是由生成图、后期加字编辑图或与政策无关的原图拼装成所谓证据，导致文本事实、图像内容和图文绑定关系同时失真，整体核心命题无法成立，应判为 False。

prediction.predicted_label: verdict=False | taxonomy=T1 Fabrication; V2 Visual Editing; C2 Contextual Inconsistency

best_trajectory.observation:
[Answer Box] As of March 2026, Americans will still be changing their clocks twice a year. No federal law has passed to end the switch.
Daylight saving time 2026: What to know about changing clocks this ...: When is daylight saving time 2026? Timeline: Daylight saving time will begin at 2 a.m. on Sunday, March 8, 2026. What is daylight saving time? (https://www.livenowfox.com/news/daylight-saving-time-2026)
Daylight saving time starts on Sunday, March 8. Clocks ... - Instagram: March 8 is the earliest possible date daylight saving time can begin, since U.S. law mandates the clocks change on the second Sunday of March. (https://www.instagram.com/p/DVJfBMmAWpW/)
Daylight saving time in the United States - Wikipedia: Daylight saving time in the United States ; 2025, March 9, November 2 ; 2026, March 8, November 1 ; 2027, March 14, November 7 ; 2028, March 12, November 5. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Daylight_saving_time_in_the_United_States)
Daylight saving time 2026 starts Sunday. Here's what time the clocks ...: The time change known as daylight savings starts Sunday, March 8, 2026. Most Americans lose an hour of sleep when clocks "spring forward." (https://www.cbsnews.com/news/daylight-savings-time-starts-2026/)
Daylight saving time in 2026 begins at 2 a.m. on Sunday, March 8 ...: Daylight saving time in 2026 begins at 2 a.m. on Sunday, March 8, when clocks "spring forward" one hour, bringing later sunsets and longer ... (https://www.facebook.com/FOX35Orlando/posts/daylight-saving-time-in-2026-begins-at-2-am-on-sunday-march-8-when-clocks-spring/1499476214871442/)

best_trajectory.thought: The search results confirm that Daylight Saving Time is still scheduled for March 8, 2026, directly contradicting the text's claim of an "official cancellation" and "emergency stop" by the White House. Additionally, the text claims the image is from Times Square, but the image clearly shows the "U.S. DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION" building. These facts refute the news text.

The news text describes an event in March 2026, which is in the future. The text also claims the image is from Times Square. I need to confirm the visual details to determine if the image is a real photo, a generated image, or if the location matches the text. The visible sign "U.S. DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION" suggests it's not Times Square. The text on the screen "Daylight Saving Time Canceled" is similar but not identical to "DST CANCELED". This suggests a mismatch and likely a fabricated image for a hypothetical scenario.
best_trajectory.observation:
图像本身有明显的“后期合成/篡改”迹象，可信度不高：

- **内容不合逻辑的场景组合**：画面像是华盛顿特区街景，楼体入口上方清楚写着 **“U.S. DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION”**，但却挂着类似时代广场/商业广告级别的超大蓝底白字告示牌“Daylight Saving Time Canceled / No Clock Change Tonight”。这种政府机关楼外立面出现如此巨幅、极简且带新闻标题风格的“公告屏”不符合常见现实布置，更像是为传播目的制作的视觉锤。
- **牌面与建筑的融合感可疑**：大屏区域在立面上显得“过于平整、干净”，边缘与周围窗格/幕墙结构的衔接缺少自然的安装结构细节（支架、框体厚度、反射与脏污一致性等），呈现出“贴上去”的观感。
- **光照与反射一致性不足**：蓝色屏幕应对周围玻璃、金属和地面产生更明显的综合色偏与反光扩散，但画面中周边反射与环境照明（街灯、室内灯）之间的相互影响看起来偏弱/不自然，像是屏幕内容后加。
- **文字呈现过于完美**：字体边缘极锐利、无可见像素结构或拍摄导致的摩尔纹/刷新条纹（现实中拍摄大型LED屏常见），更像直接渲染或后期叠字。

同时，画面整体（行人拍照、黄昏/夜景氛围、建筑透视）在摄影层面是“像真”的，但**最关键的信息载体——大屏文字内容与其物理载体**存在多处不协调，因此更倾向于经过数字编辑或合成，而非现场真实抓拍。

- **Large electronic billboard / sign** reading “Daylight Saving Time Canceled / No Clock Change Tonight” — **High certainty** (text is clearly visible).
- **U.S. Department of Education** (building entrance signage) — **High certainty** (“U.S. DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION” visible above doors).
- **Office buildings / downtown city street at night** — **High certainty** (multiple lit high-rise façades).
- **Streetlights / lampposts** — **High certainty** (visible on right side).
- **Crowd of pedestrians** (people in winter clothing) — **High certainty** (seen in foreground).
- **Smartphones/cameras held up** (people photographing the billboard) — **High certainty**.
- **The North Face logo** on a jacket (right foreground) — **Medium-high certainty** (brand mark consistent with The North Face).
- **Traffic signal** (left side) — **Medium certainty** (partially visible, typical signal housing).
- **No identifiable notable individuals** — **High certainty** (faces not visible/recognizable).

prediction.taxonomy_rationale: The text fabricates a non-existent event (DST cancellation) and misidentifies the location of the main image as Times Square despite visible signage for the U.S. Department of Education, while the images are clearly manipulated with added fake screens and news overlays to support the false narrative.
